name: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
subtitle: Renewable Energy | Biomass | Energy Management — EN ISO 18134:2017 (moisture), EN ISO 18125:2017 (calorific value)

inScope: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

enum.label: Wood pellets EN ISO 17225-2 — GCV_dry=19800 kJ/kg

inputs.label: Oxygen Content in Flue Gas (O₂, dry)

standards.name: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
standards.description: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating — Renewable Energy, Biomass, Energy Management

formulas.expression: const GCV_map = {wood_chips:19500, pellets:19800, straw:17500, miscanthus:18000, sawdust:19000}; GCV_dry_kj_kg = GCV_map[biomass_type]
formulas.reference: EN ISO 18125:2017 (calorific values); GCV on dry ash-free basis by fuel type

formulas.expression: M_frac = moisture_content_pct / 100; NCV_ar_kj_kg = GCV_dry_kj_kg * (1 - M_frac) - 2440 * (M_frac + 0.09 * H_content_dry_pct/100 * (1 - M_frac))
formulas.reference: NCV_ar = GCV_dry × (1−M) − 2440 × [M + 0.09·H_dry·(1−M)] — EN ISO 18125 / Milne equation; 2440 kJ/kg = latent heat of water vapour
ṁ_fuel = Q_boiler / (NCV_ar × η_boiler) — mass flow for steady-state heat balance
V_fuel = ṁ_fuel / ρ_bulk — fuel store sizing: minimum 3 days' storage recommended per CIBSE Guide B1

formulas.expression: XS_air_pct = O2_flue_pct * 100 / (21 - O2_flue_pct); m_air_stoich_kg_kg = 8.89 * (1 - moisture_content_pct/100); m_air_actual_kg_kg = m_air_stoich_kg_kg * (1 + XS_air_pct / 100)
formulas.reference: Stoichiometric air ≈ 8.89 kg/kg for wood C₁H₁.₄O₀.₆; XS_air = O₂/(21−O₂) × 100 — EN 303-5
Q_fan = ṁ_fuel × m_air / ρ_air; ρ_air = 1.2 kg/m³ at 20°C — combustion air fan sizing per EN 303-5 §5.4

validation.message: NCV_ar < 5000 kJ/kg: very wet fuel — combustion may not self-sustain below 35% moisture without auxiliary burner support; dry fuel below 25% moisture for reliable combustion
Pellet moisture cannot exceed 50% — EN ISO 17225-2 Class A1 pellets: M ≤ 10%; M > 50% physically impossible for pellets; check input
O₂ < 4% for straw combustion: high CO and unburned carbon risk — straw requires O₂ ≥ 6% due to irregular particle shape; increase secondary air

warnings.message: Moisture > 35%: NCV drops sharply and boiler efficiency falls significantly — each 10% moisture increase reduces NCV by ~15%; pre-dry fuel or select lower-moisture grade per EN ISO 17225
NCV_ar < 10 MJ/kg: low energy density — fuel consumption and storage volume approximately double vs dry fuel; evaluate chip drying or switch to pellets
Excess air > 80%: very high for biomass — stack losses will be excessive; target 40–60% excess air (O₂ 7–9%) for grate-fired systems per EN 303-5
Daily fuel volume > 50 m³: large biomass logistics requirement — size vehicle access, unloading equipment, and fire compartmentation per NFPA 664
Pellet boiler efficiency < 80%: below EN 303-5 Class 4 minimum for pellet appliances — verify combustion tuning, grate design, and heat exchanger fouling

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
